vraag: What is cloud computing?
opties: Storing files on remote servers only
Delivering computing services over the internet on a pay-as-you-go basis
Running software applications on a local machine
Using physical hardware infrastructure in your own data center that meets enterprise compliance requirements
toelichting: Cloud computing is the delivery of computing services (servers, storage, databases, networking, software) over the internet (the cloud) with pay-as-you-go pricing.

vraag: Which cloud deployment model uses a mix of on-premises and cloud resources?
opties: Hybrid cloud
Public cloud
Private cloud hosted internally
Community cloud for shared use by multiple organizations that meets enterprise compliance requirements
toelichting: A hybrid cloud combines public and private clouds, allowing data and applications to be shared between them.

vraag: What is a key benefit of the consumption-based model?
opties: You must sign a long-term contract for services for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
Fixed upfront costs are required before using services with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
You manage the physical hardware infrastructure yourself
You only pay for what you use
toelichting: The consumption-based model ('pay-as-you-go') means you only pay for the resources you actually use, with no upfront costs.

vraag: Which of these is a benefit of high availability in Azure?
opties: Applications run faster than before
Applications need less storage space over time with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
Applications can recover from failures with minimal downtime
Applications cost less to run overall with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
toelichting: High availability ensures applications remain running with minimal downtime, even when failures occur.

vraag: What does scalability mean in cloud computing?
opties: The ability to run applications on any device type
The ability to adjust resources as demand changes
The ability to reduce costs over time automatically
The ability to add more security features when needed across all supported deployment models
toelichting: Scalability is the ability to add or remove resources (compute, storage, etc.) as demand changes, without over-provisioning.

vraag: What is elasticity in cloud computing?
opties: Applications are deployed much faster than before
Data is automatically backed up to a secondary location
Resources are fixed and cannot change after deployment as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
Resources can automatically scale up and down based on demand
toelichting: Elasticity means the cloud can automatically scale resources up or down in response to real-time demand changes.

vraag: Which of the following is a characteristic of the public cloud?
opties: Resources are dedicated to a single organization exclusively for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
Resources are hosted entirely on-premises behind a firewall
Resources are shared by multiple organizations over the internet
Resources are only available to government agencies and partners
toelichting: In the public cloud, third-party providers make resources available to multiple organizations (multi-tenant) over the internet.

vraag: What is the primary purpose of fault tolerance?
opties: To simplify application deployment across environments
To increase application speed and reduce latency for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
To keep applications running even when components fail
To reduce storage costs by compressing data
toelichting: Fault tolerance ensures that a system continues operating even when one or more components fail, with no interruption of service.

vraag: What is the difference between CapEx and OpEx?
opties: CapEx is mainly used for software licensing; OpEx is used for hardware purchases as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
CapEx is a one-time upfront cost; OpEx is ongoing operational cost
There is no practical difference between the two models
CapEx is always variable cost; OpEx is always fixed cost that meets enterprise compliance requirements
toelichting: CapEx (Capital Expenditure) is a one-time upfront investment in physical infrastructure. OpEx (Operational Expenditure) is ongoing spending for services consumed.

vraag: What does IaaS provide?
opties: Software applications accessed via a web browser on any device
A complete business application ready to use out of the box that meets enterprise compliance requirements
Virtualized computing resources like VMs, storage, and networking
A fully managed platform for developing new applications across all supported deployment models
toelichting: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) provides virtualized compute, storage, and networking resources on demand.

vraag: What does PaaS provide?
opties: Physical servers hosted in a third-party data center facility
Ready-to-use software applications for business users
Virtual machines and basic networking connectivity only that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A managed platform to build, deploy, and scale applications
toelichting: PaaS (Platform as a Service) provides a managed environment for building, deploying, and scaling applications without managing the underlying infrastructure.

vraag: What does SaaS provide?
opties: Ready-to-use software applications over the internet
Virtualized hardware resources for running custom workloads across all supported deployment models
Cloud storage capacity for files and databases only that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A development platform for building custom applications
toelichting: SaaS (Software as a Service) delivers fully functional software applications over the internet, managed by the provider.

vraag: Which Azure service is an example of IaaS?
opties: Azure SQL Database — a managed relational database service
Microsoft 365 — a suite of productivity applications
Azure Virtual Machines — virtualized servers in the cloud
Azure App Service — a platform for hosting web applications for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
toelichting: Azure Virtual Machines is an IaaS offering, giving you full control over VMs including the OS and installed software.

vraag: Which Azure service is an example of PaaS?
opties: Azure Blob Storage for storing unstructured data at scale as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
Azure App Service for hosting web apps and REST APIs
Azure Virtual Machines for running custom workloads
Azure Firewall for network security and traffic filtering
toelichting: Azure App Service is a PaaS offering for hosting web apps, REST APIs, and mobile backends without managing the infrastructure.

vraag: Which Azure service is an example of SaaS?
opties: Microsoft 365 — productivity apps managed by Microsoft
Azure Kubernetes Service — managed container orchestration that meets enterprise compliance requirements
Azure Functions — serverless event-driven compute
Azure Virtual Desktop — desktop virtualization service
toelichting: Microsoft 365 is a SaaS offering — a complete set of productivity applications managed by Microsoft.

vraag: What is an Azure region?
opties: A virtual network created inside the Azure cloud platform with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A single data center located in a specific city
A global network of Microsoft offices around the world
A set of data centers deployed within a latency-defined perimeter
toelichting: An Azure region is a set of data centers deployed in a specific area, connected with low-latency networking.

vraag: What is the purpose of availability zones?
opties: To provide different pricing tiers for Azure services as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
To enable global load balancing across continents for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
To protect applications from data center failures
To reduce network latency across geographic regions
toelichting: Availability zones are physically separate data centers within an Azure region, protecting against a single data center failure.

vraag: What is a region pair?
opties: Two availability zones within a single Azure region
Two regions in the same geography, at least 300 miles apart
Two regions located on different continents entirely
Two regions deployed within the same country borders as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
toelichting: A region pair is two regions in the same geography, at least 300 miles apart, used for disaster recovery and high availability.

vraag: What is Azure Resource Manager (ARM)?
opties: The deployment and management service for Azure resources
A tool designed for managing physical on-premises servers with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A virtual machine management tool with monitoring features
A fully managed database service available in Azure with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
toelichting: Azure Resource Manager is the management layer that enables you to create, update, and delete resources in your Azure subscription.

vraag: What is an Azure resource group?
opties: A logical grouping of Azure subscriptions under one account that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A pricing tier that determines how much you pay for services for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
A collection of virtual machines deployed in the same region
A container that holds related resources for an Azure solution
toelichting: A resource group is a logical container for grouping related Azure resources that share the same lifecycle.

vraag: What is an Azure subscription?
opties: A specific type of virtual machine available in Azure
A networking service that connects on-premises to Azure with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A logical container for resource groups that defines billing boundaries
A single Azure resource deployed in the cloud for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
toelichting: An Azure subscription is a logical unit of Azure services linked to an Azure account. It defines billing and access boundaries.

vraag: What is a management group in Azure?
opties: A group of related virtual networks in the same region that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A group of users managed in Azure Active Directory
A specific pricing tier for enterprise customers only that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A container for managing access, policies, and compliance across multiple subscriptions
toelichting: Management groups help organize and manage multiple subscriptions at scale, applying governance policies across them.

vraag: Which Azure service provides serverless compute?
opties: Azure Kubernetes Service — managed container orchestration
Azure Virtual Machines — virtualized servers in the cloud across all supported deployment models
Azure App Service — platform for hosting web applications
Azure Functions — event-driven serverless compute service
toelichting: Azure Functions is a serverless compute service that runs event-driven code without requiring you to manage infrastructure.

vraag: What is Azure Blob Storage used for?
opties: Hosting web applications and API endpoints
Storing large amounts of unstructured data such as images and videos
Running virtual machine disks for production workloads
Storing structured relational data in tables that meets enterprise compliance requirements
toelichting: Azure Blob Storage is designed for storing massive amounts of unstructured data, such as text, binary data, images, and videos.

vraag: What is Azure SQL Database?
opties: A NoSQL database service for document storage
An in-memory cache for high-performance applications
A fully managed relational database service based on SQL Server
A key-value store for simple lookups and queries across all supported deployment models
toelichting: Azure SQL Database is a fully managed Platform as a Service (PaaS) relational database based on Microsoft SQL Server.

vraag: What is Cosmos DB in Azure?
opties: A globally distributed NoSQL database service
A caching service for improving application performance as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
A relational database for structured data storage
A storage account service for blobs and files
toelichting: Azure Cosmos DB is a globally distributed, multi-model NoSQL database service with low latency and high availability.

vraag: What is the purpose of Azure Virtual Network (VNet)?
opties: To store virtual machine disks and snapshots across all supported deployment models
To deploy web applications to the internet
To monitor network traffic and performance metrics
To logically isolate and connect Azure resources in a network
toelichting: Azure Virtual Network enables Azure resources to securely communicate with each other, the internet, and on-premises networks.

vraag: What is Azure Load Balancer used for?
opties: Distributing incoming network traffic across multiple VMs
Blocking malicious traffic from reaching your applications for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
Managing DNS records and domain name resolution
Storing configuration files for your applications with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
toelichting: Azure Load Balancer distributes incoming traffic to healthy backend resources (VMs or VM scale sets) to ensure high availability.

vraag: What is Azure Active Directory (Azure AD)?
opties: A fully managed database service running in Azure
Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service
A virtual network service for connecting cloud resources
A directory service designed for on-premises servers only as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
toelichting: Azure AD is Microsoft's cloud-based identity and access management service, providing authentication and authorization for cloud resources.

vraag: What is the principle of least privilege?
opties: Granting permissions only to senior administrators
Removing all permissions from all users at all times for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
Granting only the minimum permissions needed to perform a task
Giving every user all available permissions by default
toelichting: The principle of least privilege means giving users only the permissions they absolutely need to do their job, nothing more.

vraag: What is RBAC in Azure?
opties: A role-based access control system for managing permissions on Azure resources
A billing management tool for tracking cloud spending with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A networking protocol for secure data transmission as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
A database backup and recovery tool for Azure SQL
toelichting: Azure RBAC (Role-Based Access Control) lets you manage who has access to Azure resources, what they can do, and which resources they can access.

vraag: What is Azure Policy used for?
opties: Managing virtual machine backups and disaster recovery across all supported deployment models
Deploying containerized applications using Kubernetes
Monitoring application performance and setting alerts
Creating and enforcing governance rules for your Azure resources
toelichting: Azure Policy helps enforce organizational standards and assess compliance by creating rules that govern resource configurations.

vraag: What is the defense-in-depth security model?
opties: A layered approach to security with multiple defensive measures
A firewall-only strategy for protecting network traffic
A security model designed for physical data centers exclusively
A single layer of security to protect cloud resources across all supported deployment models
toelichting: Defense in depth uses multiple layers of security (physical, identity, network, application, data) so that if one layer fails, others still provide protection.

vraag: What is Azure Blueprints?
opties: A diagramming tool for designing network architectures with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A tool for designing relational database schemas
A service for defining a repeatable set of Azure resources and policies
A service for creating virtual machines from templates
toelichting: Azure Blueprints enables you to define a repeatable set of Azure resources, policies, and role assignments for creating compliant environments.

vraag: What does Azure Security Center provide?
opties: Unified security management and threat protection across hybrid cloud workloads
Cost management and billing optimization recommendations that meets enterprise compliance requirements
Domain name system (DNS) management and resolution
Container orchestration using Kubernetes technology that meets enterprise compliance requirements
toelichting: Azure Security Center provides unified security management, advanced threat protection, and security recommendations across Azure and on-premises workloads.

vraag: What is a Service Level Agreement (SLA) in Azure?
opties: A pricing agreement that determines service costs with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A technical support plan for enterprise customers
A contract that guarantees specific application features
Microsoft's commitment regarding uptime and connectivity for Azure services
toelichting: An SLA is Microsoft's formal commitment to a specific level of service availability (uptime percentage) and connectivity for each Azure service.

vraag: What is the Azure Pricing Calculator used for?
opties: Tracking your actual Azure spending after deployment as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
Applying discounts and credits to your monthly bill
Estimating the cost of Azure services before deploying them
Generating invoices for existing Azure resources
toelichting: The Azure Pricing Calculator lets you estimate the cost of Azure services to help you plan and budget before deployment.

vraag: What is the Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) Calculator?
opties: A tool to monitor your current Azure costs in real time across all supported deployment models
A tool to estimate the cost of running an application on-premises vs. in Azure
A tool to estimate required network bandwidth for applications with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A tool to configure virtual machine sizes and specifications
toelichting: The TCO Calculator compares the cost of your current on-premises infrastructure with the cost of running the same workload in Azure.

vraag: Which factor affects the cost of an Azure resource?
opties: The total number of users in your organization with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
The color theme selected in the Azure portal interface
The resource type and its size configuration only across all supported deployment models
The Azure region, resource size, and usage duration
toelichting: Azure costs depend on the resource type, its configuration (size), the region where it runs, and how long it's used.

vraag: What is Azure Advisor?
opties: A help desk ticketing system for reporting issues
A personalized recommendation engine for best practices in Azure
A virtual machine management and monitoring tool for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
A service that automatically deploys applications to Azure that meets enterprise compliance requirements
toelichting: Azure Advisor analyzes your deployed resources and gives personalized recommendations on cost, performance, reliability, and security.

vraag: What is Azure Monitor?
opties: A billing and cost management tool for Azure
A network security and traffic filtering service across all supported deployment models
A service for collecting, analyzing, and acting on telemetry from Azure resources
A service for deploying and managing applications
toelichting: Azure Monitor provides full observability (metrics, logs, alerts) into the performance and health of your applications and infrastructure.

vraag: What is Azure Service Health?
opties: A database health monitoring and performance tool
A tool for monitoring on-premises server infrastructure as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
A service that provides information about service issues and planned maintenance
A dashboard that shows the health of your specific deployed applications
toelichting: Azure Service Health provides a personalized view of Azure service health, including ongoing issues, planned maintenance, and advisory information.

vraag: What is Azure Resource Locks?
opties: Locks that prevent accidental deletion or modification of Azure resources
Locks that encrypt data stored in Azure at rest
Locks that restrict network traffic to and from resources across all supported deployment models
Locks that prevent unauthorized users from logging in
toelichting: Resource locks prevent accidental deletion or modification of critical Azure resources by applying a CanNotDelete or ReadOnly lock.

vraag: What does Azure Key Vault do?
opties: Manages user passwords and login credentials only across all supported deployment models
Monitors network traffic for security threats
Encrypts all data stored in Azure automatically across all supported deployment models
Stores and manages secrets, encryption keys, and certificates
toelichting: Azure Key Vault securely stores and manages secrets (API keys, passwords), encryption keys, and certificates.

vraag: What is Microsoft Defender for Cloud?
opties: A DNS protection service for domain security
A backup service for virtual machines and databases that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A firewall service for blocking malicious traffic that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A cloud workload protection platform with security alerts and advanced threat protection
toelichting: Microsoft Defender for Cloud provides unified security management and advanced threat protection across hybrid and multi-cloud workloads.

vraag: What is a Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure AD) tenant?
opties: A dedicated and trusted instance of Microsoft Entra ID for an organization
A security group that defines user permissions and roles
A single user account with access to Azure resources that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A virtual network that isolates cloud resources as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
toelichting: A Microsoft Entra ID tenant is a dedicated directory representing an organization, used for identity and access management.

vraag: What is Conditional Access in Microsoft Entra ID?
opties: A feature that blocks all access requests by default
A networking feature for establishing VPN connections across all supported deployment models
A tool for evaluating access conditions before granting access
A billing feature for applying conditional pricing discounts
toelichting: Conditional Access evaluates signals (user, device, location, risk) to enforce access policies, such as requiring MFA for specific users.

vraag: What is Azure Firewall?
opties: A virtual private network service for remote access across all supported deployment models
A web application firewall that only protects HTTP traffic for optimal performance and scalability across all workloads
A managed network security service that protects Azure Virtual Network resources
A physical firewall appliance deployed in Microsoft data centers
toelichting: Azure Firewall is a managed, cloud-based network security service that protects your Azure Virtual Network resources.

vraag: What is DDoS Protection in Azure?
opties: A traffic management service for load balancing as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
A firewall designed specifically for web applications that meets enterprise compliance requirements
A service that blocks all incoming network traffic
A service that protects applications from Distributed Denial of Service attacks
toelichting: Azure DDoS Protection safeguards Azure applications by monitoring and mitigating Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks.

vraag: What is the Azure Well-Architected Framework?
opties: A set of design principles for building secure, reliable, and cost-effective cloud solutions
A compliance certification standard for cloud applications as part of a comprehensive cloud strategy
A detailed framework for designing physical data centers with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
A blueprint for designing network architecture in Azure
toelichting: The Azure Well-Architected Framework provides guiding principles across five pillars: cost optimization, performance efficiency, reliability, security, and operational excellence.

vraag: What does the Azure Marketplace offer?
opties: Free Azure subscriptions for new customers with built-in support for monitoring and analytics
Physical hardware for on-premises data centers
A catalog of third-party solutions and services certified to run on Azure
Only Microsoft-owned software and services
toelichting: Azure Marketplace is an online store with thousands of third-party applications, services, and solutions certified to run on Azure.
